job_title.description: The user's current or most recent professional role.

industry.description: The industry or sector the user has primarily worked in.

years_experience.description: Approximate total years of professional work experience.

education_level.type: enum: high_school | associate | bachelor | master | doctoral | other
education_level.description: The highest level of formal education completed.

session_goal.description: Whether the user wants to explore career options broadly or pursue a specific target role.

location.description: City / region / remote preference. Plan calls for explicit location capture so downstream research can filter regional wage and job-count signals. (C1)

preferred_timeline.description: User-stated transition horizon — months, quarters, or 'undecided'. Feeds the planning timeline without being overwritten by it. (C1)

track.description: System-tracked field indicating which exploration track is active.

interests.description: Topics, fields, or activities the user finds engaging or wants to explore.

constraints.description: Location, schedule, compensation, or other practical constraints on career choices.

candidate_directions.description: 2-4 potential career directions surfaced during exploration, each with title and rationale.

direction_title.description: Name of the candidate career direction.

rationale.description: Brief explanation of why this direction fits the user's profile.

target_role.description: The specific job role the user wants to pursue.

skills.description: Skills required for the target role with self-assessment ratings.

skill_name.description: Name of the skill from O*NET.

onet_source.description: O*NET element ID or reference for this skill.

required_proficiency.description: Level of proficiency typically required for the target role.

user_rating.description: User's self-assessed proficiency level on a 4-point scale.

gap_category.description: Derived gap category based on comparing user_rating to required_proficiency.

learning_needs.description: User-identified priority areas for skill development, captured post-assessment.

learning_needs_complete.description: True once learning needs and timeframe have been discussed post-assessment.

skills_evaluation_summary.description: Brief textual summary of the gap analysis presented to the user for confirmation.

user_confirmed_evaluation.description: True once the user explicitly confirms the skills evaluation summary is accurate.

recommended_path.description: The synthesized recommended career path based on gap analysis.

timeline.description: Estimated timeline for skill development and career transition.

skill_development_agenda.description: Ordered list of skill development priorities and resources.

immediate_next_steps.description: 2-3 concrete actions the user can take right away.

plan_rationale.description: Explanation of why this plan suits the user's profile and goals.

report_generated.description: Whether a PDF/HTML career plan report has been exported.

learning_resources.description: Curated links (title, url, optional note) suggested for the user's plan.

evidence_kept.description: Data sources or facts retained for the plan with reasons.

plan_blocks.description: Block-by-block plan confirmation (Sr 31, 32). Fixed ids: understanding, path, skills, courses, end_goal. Each must be confirmed before the next is surfaced.

id.description: One of: understanding | path | skills | courses | end_goal

shift_intent.description: Sr 28: user wishes to completely shift career. Activates the shift advisory variant of planning (financial/emotional caveat first, then re-evaluate experience reuse).

evidence_discarded.description: Sources or claims set aside with reasons. A non-empty `reason` is REQUIRED on every entry (G7); the state-updater drops items missing it and logs STATE_SCHEMA_VIOLATION.

reason.description: Why it was not used (REQUIRED, non-empty)
